steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: Two independent clauses are connected by the transitional phrase “on the contrary.”

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Use a semicolon before a conjunctive adverb (like “on the contrary”) and a comma after it. Choice D follows this rule exactly. Choices A and C lack the semicolon, while B misplaces it after “on the contrary” instead of before it. That matches choice D — D is correct.
